title: mark_session_end
description: Close the currently open session, optionally attach a summary, and return final session metadata. Closes only the most recent open session for the user; explicitly does not take a session id to avoid the LLM having to maintain state across calls.

summary.description: Optional plain-language summary of what was actually accomplished. Stored verbatim alongside the original intent for later reflection skills (e.g. weekly_rollup).
examples: shipped the RFC reply, parked two follow-ups in inbox
got distracted into refactor; did not finish the planned migration ADR

session_id.description: UUIDv4 of the session that was just closed.

duration.description: ISO 8601 duration between started_at and ended_at.

intent.description: The stated intent recorded at mark_session_start, echoed back unmodified.

summary.description: The summary as stored, or null if none was provided.

NO_OPEN_SESSION.description: No session was open at the time of the call. Returned instead of silently no-op so callers can distinguish 'I forgot to start one' from 'this succeeded'.

input.summary: shipped the RFC reply, parked two follow-ups in inbox

output.summary: shipped the RFC reply, parked two follow-ups in inbox

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. The deliberate choice to not accept a session_id input means we may NEVER add one later without a major bump, since callers would then need to maintain state. This is documented in ADR 0001.
compatibility.side_effects: Mutates server-local session state (closes the open session). Idempotent only in the sense that a second call returns NO_OPEN_SESSION rather than mutating further.
compatibility.idempotency: Calling twice in succession: first call closes; second call returns NO_OPEN_SESSION.
compatibility.privacy: Summary strings are sensitive (reveal user accomplishments, frustrations, self-talk) and MUST NOT be transmitted off-device by this server.
